name: CesiumJS 3D viewer (meshed cars on 3D SF)
role: The headline 3D deliverable: replay logged car poses as meshed cars driving the real 3D San Francisco — Cesium World Terrain (hills) + Cesium OSM Buildings — with a time-dynamic clock and a verifier-driven HUD.
built: smoothride/demo/export_cesium.py packs per-car (lon,lat,height,heading,speed,crash) + the deterministic verifier summary into trajectories.json (no geometry baked in). smoothride/demo/cesium/ (index.html, app.js, config.example.js) renders box-meshed cars draped onto Cesium terrain (heights sampled from the live terrain provider), oriented by velocity, colored by speed, with a graceful no-token fallback. Runs offline over a static http server.
inputs: Cesium ion token (free) for terrain + 3D buildings
outputs: browser replay of meshed cars on 3D SF terrain + buildings
render_vs_sim: Cesium DRAWS the world (terrain + buildings stream from ion); it never computes dynamics or collisions. The compute layer emits poses, the verifier judges them, Cesium only renders.
risks: needs a free Cesium ion token -> config.example.js + graceful fallback message
orthometric DEM z vs ellipsoidal terrain -> sample Cesium terrain at runtime (fallback: z - geoid)
must be served over http (fetch fails on file://)
run: cp smoothride/demo/cesium/config.example.js smoothride/demo/cesium/config.js  # paste ion token
